title: eragrostis pilosa var. minor
intro: Eragrostis pilosa var. minor, also known as the hairy lovegrass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Eragrostis pilosa var. minor include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as providing relief from fever and other respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
